docs.title: codonanalyzer
docs.text:
Note

codonanalyzer is a Nextflow DSL2 pipeline that runs codon usage, ORF detection, translation, and hydropathy analysis for each record in a DNA multi-FASTA file.

Tip

For production runs on 96 cores / 1 TB RAM, use the hpc profile and tune conf/resources.config if your SLURM partition has stricter limits.

docs.title: End-to-end workflow
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[params.input multi-FASTA] --> B[SPLIT_FASTA]
    B --> C[CODON_ANALYSIS]
    B --> D[LONG_ORF]
    B --> E[TRANSLATE_FASTA]
    E --> F[HYDROPATHY_PROFILE]
    F --> G[PLOT_HYDROPATHY]
    C --> H[GATHER_RESULTS]
    D --> H
    E --> H
    F --> H
    G --> H
    H --> I[Final outputs in params.outdir]

Warning

Inputs with extremely long records can increase runtime in CODON_ANALYSIS and LONG_ORF because both scripts process complete sequence content.

docs.title: About
docs.text:
CodonAnalyzer is a modular bioinformatics pipeline originally developed as a Perl project in 2015 at JUIT, India, as part of their 6th-semester coursework in Programming Languages for Bioinformatics.

The current version (2025) modernizes and integrates the original Perl tools using a Nextflow DSL2 workflow. It adds standardized outputs, schema-validated parameters, profile-based runtime configuration, Python-based plotting, and support for reproducible research environments.

This evolution reflects the principles of modern scientific programming — reproducibility, modularity, and transparency — while honoring its academic roots.

To view the original report submitted in 2015, please refer to the original report.

docs.title: Author
docs.text: Abhinav Mishra Email: mishraabhinav36@gmail.com

• Migrated from Snakemake to Nextflow DSL2.
  • Added nf-core-style profile/config layout under conf/.
  • Added schema-driven parameter validation (nf-schema).
  • Expanded MkDocs documentation and Perl API reference.

docs.title: Code of Conduct
docs.text:
We are committed to a respectful, inclusive, and collaborative project environment.

  • Be respectful in all interactions.
  • Provide constructive technical feedback.
  • Report inappropriate behavior to: mishraabhinav36@gmail.com.

Note

This project follows community-first, science-reproducibility principles.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
Tip

Keep process names stable in main.nf so documentation DAGs remain accurate.

docs.title: Development rules
docs.text:
1. Do not change biological logic in Perl scripts without dedicated review.
  2. Keep nextflow_schema.json synchronized with nextflow.config params.
  3. Validate docs and pipeline changes together.

docs.title: Local checks
docs.text:
Install the docs dependencies first:

pip install -r docs/requirements.txt

Then validate the pipeline and docs:

nextflow run main.nf -profile test
mkdocs build

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Note

The pipeline entrypoint is main.nf, with runtime settings in nextflow.config and profile-specific overrides under conf/.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Nextflow >=24.04.0
  • Java 17+
  • Perl 5
  • Python 3 (for scripts/plot_hydro.py)

docs.title: Run profiles
docs.text:
LocalCondaSLURM 
nextflow run main.nf -profile standard --input data/in.fasta --outdir results

nextflow run main.nf -profile conda --input data/in.fasta --outdir results

nextflow run main.nf -profile hpc --input data/in.fasta --outdir results

Danger

Do not point --outdir to a directory with irreplaceable files, because pipeline publishing uses copy operations to materialize outputs.

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
Final merged outputs are written to:

  • ${params.outdir}/codonanalyzer_results/metrics.txt
  • ${params.outdir}/codonanalyzer_results/orf
  • ${params.outdir}/codonanalyzer_results/orf.fasta
  • ${params.outdir}/codonanalyzer_results/translated.fasta
  • ${params.outdir}/codonanalyzer_results/hplot.txt
  • ${params.outdir}/codonanalyzer_results/hsummary.txt
  • ${params.outdir}/codonanalyzer_results/*.hplot.png
  • ${params.outdir}/codonanalyzer_results/per_record/*

Warning

Combined files are concatenated from per-record outputs; if downstream tools require sorting, sort post-pipeline using a stable record identifier.

flowchart LR
    A[Per-record outputs] --> B[GATHER_RESULTS]
    B --> C[Combined outputs]

docs.title: Output Structure
docs.text:
All outputs are placed in the results/ directory:

  • metrics.txt – Codon usage and GC content
  • orf – ORF(s) summary file
  • orf.fasta – FASTA of longest ORF(s)
  • translated.fasta – Protein sequences
  • hplot.txt – Raw hydropathy values
  • hsummary.txt – Hydropathy statistics
  • hplot.png – Histogram plot

docs.title: Parameters.generated
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description input string data/in.fasta Path to input DNA multi-FASTA file (up to 1000 headers). outdir string results Output directory for final and intermediate pipeline results. max_cpus integer 96 Maximum CPUs available to the pipeline executor. max_memory string 1000.GB Maximum memory available to pipeline processes. max_time string 240.h Maximum walltime available to pipeline processes. help boolean false Print help text generated by nf-schema plugin.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
Note

Parameter validation and help are driven by nextflow_schema.json using the nf-schema plugin in main.nf and nextflow.config.

Tip

Regenerate parameter documentation from schema whenever parameter definitions change.

Parameter Type Default Description input string data/in.fasta Path to input DNA multi-FASTA file (up to 1000 headers). outdir string results Output directory for final and intermediate pipeline results. max_cpus integer 96 Maximum CPUs available to the pipeline executor. max_memory string 1000.GB Maximum memory available to pipeline processes. max_time string 240.h Maximum walltime available to pipeline processes. help boolean false Print help text generated by nf-schema plugin.

docs.title: Parameter DAG context
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[input] --> B[SPLIT_FASTA]
    C[max_cpus/max_memory/max_time] --> D[Process resources]
    E[outdir] --> F[publishDir targets]

docs.title: Pipeline Overview
docs.text:
Note

The DAG below reflects actual process names from main.nf.

flowchart LR
    I[Input multi-FASTA] --> S[SPLIT_FASTA]
    S --> C[CODON_ANALYSIS]
    S --> L[LONG_ORF]
    S --> T[TRANSLATE_FASTA]
    T --> H[HYDROPATHY_PROFILE]
    H --> P[PLOT_HYDROPATHY]
    C --> G[GATHER_RESULTS]
    L --> G
    T --> G
    H --> G
    P --> G

docs.title: What each stage does
docs.text:
• SPLIT_FASTA: splits multi-FASTA into single-record FASTA files.
  • CODON_ANALYSIS: runs scripts/codon.pl per record.
  • LONG_ORF: runs scripts/longORF.pl per record.
  • TRANSLATE_FASTA: runs scripts/translate.pl per record.
  • HYDROPATHY_PROFILE: runs scripts/hydropathy.pl per record.
  • PLOT_HYDROPATHY: runs scripts/plot_hydro.py per record.
  • GATHER_RESULTS: merges all per-record outputs into final files.

Tip

Intermediate files are published under ${params.outdir}/intermediate/ for auditability.

docs.title: SPLIT_FASTA
docs.text:
Note

Input: params.input multi-FASTA.

flowchart LR
    A[params.input] --> B[SPLIT_FASTA]
    B --> C[*.fa per record]

docs.title: CODON_ANALYSIS (scripts/codon.pl)
docs.text:
Warning

This process computes many metrics, including codon-level and entropy statistics.

flowchart LR
    A[record.fa] --> B[CODON_ANALYSIS]
    B --> C[id.metrics.txt]

docs.title: LONG_ORF (scripts/longORF.pl)
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[record.fa] --> B[LONG_ORF]
    B --> C[id.orf]
    B --> D[id.orf.fasta]

docs.title: TRANSLATE_FASTA (scripts/translate.pl)
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[record.fa] --> B[TRANSLATE_FASTA]
    B --> C[id.translated.fasta]

docs.title: HYDROPATHY_PROFILE (scripts/hydropathy.pl)
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[id.translated.fasta] --> B[HYDROPATHY_PROFILE]
    B --> C[id.hplot.txt]
    B --> D[id.hsummary.txt]

docs.title: PLOT_HYDROPATHY (scripts/plot_hydro.py)
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[id.hplot.txt] --> B[PLOT_HYDROPATHY]
    B --> C[id.hplot.png]

docs.title: GATHER_RESULTS
docs.text:
Danger

This is the terminal merge stage and rewrites combined outputs under ${params.outdir}/codonanalyzer_results/.

flowchart LR
    A[metrics/orf/translated/hplot/hsummary/png] --> B[GATHER_RESULTS]
    B --> C[metrics.txt]
    B --> D[orf + orf.fasta]
    B --> E[translated.fasta]
    B --> F[hplot.txt + hsummary.txt]
    B --> G[hplot PNGs]

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text:
1. Prepare your FASTA file Place your input file (e.g. in.fasta) in a data directory.

  2. Configure the pipeline Edit config.yaml: ```yaml fasta: path/to/in.fasta scripts_dir: scripts

  3. Run the pipeline Execute the Snakemake command to start the pipeline: 

    snakemake --cores 1
    or 
    snakemake --cores 1 --config fasta=alt.fasta scripts_dir=myscripts
    This will run the pipeline using 1 core and the specified FASTA file and scripts directory.

docs.title: References
docs.text:
The development and interpretation of this pipeline were influenced by foundational research in genomics and hydropathy analysis:

  1. Galtier, N. & Lobry, J.R. (1997). "Relationships between genomic G+C content, RNA secondary structures, and optimal growth temperature in prokaryotes." J Mol Evol, 44(6): 632–636.

  2. Hurst, L.D. & Merchant, A.R. (2001). "High guanine–cytosine content is not an adaptation to high temperature: a comparative analysis amongst prokaryotes." Proc. R. Soc. B, 268(1466): 493–497.

  3. Ilatovskiy, A., Petukhov, M. "Genome-wide search for local DNA segments with anomalous GC-content."

  4. Kyte, J. & Doolittle, R.F. (1982). "A simple method for displaying the hydropathic character of a protein." J Mol Biol, 157(1): 105–132.

  5. Makino, K., et al. (1999). "Complete nucleotide sequence of the prophage VT2-Sakai..." Genes Genet Syst, 74(5): 227–239.

  6. Welch, R.A., et al. (2002). "Extensive mosaic structure revealed by the complete genome sequence of uropathogenic E. coli." Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. USA, 99(26): 17020–17024.

  7. Šmarda, P., et al. (2014). "Genomic GC content diversity in monocots." Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci., 111(39): E4096–E4102.

  8. Yakovchuk, P., et al. (2006). "Base-stacking and base-pairing contributions into thermal stability of the DNA double helix." Nucleic Acids Res, 34(2): 564–574.

  9. Jamison, C. (2004). Perl Programming for Bioinformatics & Biologists, Wiley DreamTech.

  10. Moorhouse, M., et al. (2004). Bioinformatics, Biocomputing and PERL, Wiley & Sons.

  11. Dwyer, R.A. (2003). Genomic Perl: From Bioinformatics Basics to Working Code, Cambridge University Press.

  12. Mölder F, Jablonski KP, Letcher B et al. Sustainable data analysis with Snakemake [version 1; peer review: 1 approved, 1 approved with reservations]. F1000Research 2021, 10:33 (https://doi.org/10.12688/f1000research.29032.1)

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Tip

Use -profile test for smoke checks before full-scale datasets.

docs.title: Basic invocation
docs.text:
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile standard \
  --input data/in.fasta \
  --outdir results

docs.title: Recommended production invocation (SLURM)
docs.text:
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile hpc \
  --input /path/to/input.fasta \
  --outdir /path/to/output \
  --max_cpus 96 \
  --max_memory 1000.GB \
  --max_time 240.h

Warning

The pipeline splits records first, so very high header counts (close to 1000) can generate many short jobs and increase scheduler overhead.

docs.title: Stage graph
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    U1[Run command] --> U2[SPLIT_FASTA]
    U2 --> U3[Per-record fanout]
    U3 --> U4[CODON_ANALYSIS / LONG_ORF / TRANSLATE_FASTA]
    U4 --> U5[HYDROPATHY_PROFILE]
    U5 --> U6[PLOT_HYDROPATHY]
    U4 --> U7[GATHER_RESULTS]
    U5 --> U7
    U6 --> U7

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
This section documents the four core Perl analysis scripts used by the codonanalyzer pipeline.

Script Description codon.pl Compute codon usage, nucleotide composition, RSCU, ENc, ORF statistics, and 3-mer Shannon entropy from a DNA FASTA record. longORF.pl Identify the longest open reading frames (ORFs) in all six translation frames of a FASTA sequence. translate.pl Translate DNA FASTA sequences into protein sequences using the standard genetic code. hydropathy.pl Compute per-residue and sliding-window Kyte–Doolittle hydropathy scores for translated protein sequences.

docs.title: codon.pl
docs.text: Author: Abhinav Mishra

docs.title: Name
docs.text: codon.pl — compute codon usage and nucleotide composition metrics for one FASTA record.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: perl scripts/codon.pl <in.fasta> <out.txt>

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Calculates nucleotide counts, GC/AT percentages and skews, dinucleotide/codon counts, RSCU, ENc, amino-acid composition, ORF statistics, sliding-window GC, and 3-mer entropy.

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• in.fasta: input FASTA with one sequence record.
  • out.txt: metrics output text file.

docs.title: Input/Output
docs.text:
• Input: single-record DNA FASTA.
  • Output: plain-text metrics report (*.metrics.txt).

docs.title: Author
docs.text: Abhinav Mishra (mishraabhinav36@gmail.com)

docs.title: Copyright and License
docs.text: Copyright (c) 2025 Abhinav Mishra. BSD 3-Clause.

docs.title: hydropathy.pl
docs.text: Author: Abhinav Mishra

docs.title: Name
docs.text: hydropathy.pl — calculate residue-level hydropathy and summary statistics.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: perl scripts/hydropathy.pl <in.fasta> <H_PLOT.txt> <H_SUMMARY.txt>

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Computes Kyte–Doolittle hydropathy values for protein FASTA records and writes detailed and summary tabular outputs.

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• in.fasta: translated protein FASTA input.
  • H_PLOT.txt: per-residue hydropathy values output.
  • H_SUMMARY.txt: sequence summary metrics output.

docs.title: Input/Output
docs.text:
• Input: single-record protein FASTA.
  • Output: hydropathy detail (*.hplot.txt) and summary (*.hsummary.txt).

docs.title: Author
docs.text: Abhinav Mishra (mishraabhinav36@gmail.com)

docs.title: Copyright and License
docs.text: Copyright (c) 2025 Abhinav Mishra. BSD 3-Clause.

docs.title: longORF.pl
docs.text: Author: Abhinav Mishra

docs.title: Name
docs.text: longORF.pl — identify the longest ORF(s) in FASTA input.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: perl scripts/longORF.pl <in.fasta> <out.table> [whole]

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Scans forward and reverse-complement strands for ORFs using canonical start/stop codons, writes summary table and ORF FASTA.

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• in.fasta: input FASTA with one sequence record.
  • out.table: ORF summary table output.
  • whole (optional): emit whole sequence to FASTA output.

docs.title: Input/Output
docs.text:
• Input: single-record DNA FASTA.
  • Output: ORF table (*.orf) and FASTA (*.orf.fasta).

docs.title: Author
docs.text: Abhinav Mishra (mishraabhinav36@gmail.com)

docs.title: Copyright and License
docs.text: Copyright (c) 2025 Abhinav Mishra. BSD 3-Clause.

docs.title: translate.pl
docs.text: Author: Abhinav Mishra

docs.title: Name
docs.text: translate.pl — translate DNA FASTA records into protein FASTA records.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: perl scripts/translate.pl <input.fasta> [output.fasta]

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Applies standard codon translation from DNA sequence to amino-acid sequence, dropping non-ACGT characters and stop symbols.

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• input.fasta: input FASTA with one sequence record.
  • output.fasta: output translated FASTA.

docs.title: Input/Output
docs.text:
• Input: single-record DNA FASTA.
  • Output: protein FASTA (*.translated.fasta).

docs.title: Author
docs.text: Abhinav Mishra (mishraabhinav36@gmail.com)

docs.title: Copyright and License
docs.text: Copyright (c) 2025 Abhinav Mishra. BSD 3-Clause.
